items.question: ___ are loyal animals. (in general)
explanations: 'The dogs' points to specific dogs, not dogs in general.
Correct! General plurals take no article: 'Dogs are loyal animals'.
'A' can't go with a plural noun.
'An' can't go with a plural noun.

items.question: ___ is essential for life. (in general)
explanations: 'The water' would mean specific water.
'A water' — water is uncountable, so no 'a'.
Correct! Uncountables in general take no article: 'Water is essential'.
'An water' is wrong — water is uncountable.

items.question: I usually have ___ at eight.
explanations: Correct! Meals take no article: 'have breakfast'.
'The breakfast' would need a specific one.
'A breakfast' only works when described (a big breakfast).
'An breakfast' is wrong.

items.question: The children go to ___ by bus.
explanations: Correct! 'Go to school' (as students) takes no article.
'The school' means the building, changing the meaning.
'A school' would mean any school.
'An school' is wrong.

items.question: ___ lives in ___ . (a person and a country)
explanations: Names and most countries take no article.
Correct! 'Sara lives in Italy' — no article with names or most countries.
Names and countries don't take a/an.
'Italy' doesn't take 'the' (unlike the USA).

items.question: ___ can't buy happiness.
explanations: 'The money' would be specific money.
'A money' — money is uncountable.
Correct! Uncountable in general: 'Money can't buy happiness'.
'An money' is wrong.

items.question: She's learning ___ at university.
explanations: Correct! Languages take no article: 'learning Japanese'.
'The Japanese' would mean the Japanese people.
Languages don't take 'a'.
Languages don't take 'an'.

items.question: Correct this: 'I go to the bed at eleven.'
options: I go to bed at eleven.
I go to a bed at eleven.
I go to an bed at eleven.
explanations: Correct! The fixed phrase is 'go to bed' — no article.
'A bed' changes it to a random bed.
'An bed' is wrong.
'Go to the bed' wrongly adds 'the'.

items.question: Which sentence talks about music IN GENERAL?
explanations: Correct! General uncountable: 'I love music' (no article).
'The music' means specific music (e.g. at a party).
'A music' — music is uncountable.
'An music' is wrong.

items.question: When do you use the zero article (no article)?
options: With a singular countable first mention.
explanations: Correct! General plurals and uncountables take no article.
A specific, known noun takes 'the'.
A first-mention singular countable takes a/an.
Superlatives take 'the'.
